The 16 Day Prep: What We Did

The seller handed me the keys.

16 days later, this San Jose home went from "needs work" to clean, move-in ready, and market-ready.

What we did:

  • Interior paint: walls, ceilings, trim, doors, and closets.
  • Exterior paint: stucco, trim, eaves, doors, and garage door.
  • Electrical: replaced ceiling lights, outlets, and switches. Repaired outlets and broken recessed lights.
  • Fireplace: removed the gold cover and painted the interior black.
  • Bathroom: added frameless shower glass and reglazed the tub.
  • Repairs: fixed fencing and rotted exterior wood.
  • Removed old window screens.
  • Handled trash-out inside and out.
  • Deep cleaned the home.
  • Refreshed curb appeal with mulch, trimming, edging, and yard cleanup.
  • Completed inspections.
  • Staged, photographed, and launched the listing.
  • Licensed trades: C-33 painter. C-10 electrician.

Prep work cost: $18k. Staging handled separately.

What We Skipped: These wouldn't have paid off here.

  • No kitchen remodel.
  • No bathroom remodel.
  • No new flooring.
  • No HVAC replacement.
  • No roof replacement.
  • No inspection repairs.
  • No full renovation.

What a seller said

"He talked us out of repainting the kitchen cabinets and replacing the counters because the whole kitchen layout is dated. His point was that a buyer would most likely redo the kitchen anyway, so spending money there would be wasted. Glad we met with him first."

San Jose Seller
